title: https://github.com/BrotherSupport/ai_consultant
acceptance_criteria: Codebase is refactored to improve readability and maintainability without altering existing functionality.
All existing tests pass successfully after the refactor.
No performance degradation is observed post-refactor.
Code adheres to the project's style guidelines as defined in scalastyle-config.xml.
technical_notes: Focus on simplifying complex methods and reducing code duplication. Pay attention to the dependency management in pom.xml to ensure no unnecessary dependencies are included. Consider using the pre-commit hooks defined in .pre-commit-config.yaml to catch any style issues early. Be cautious of any changes that might affect the public API or backward compatibility.

title: add e2e test
business_req: Ensure the reliability and stability of the Spark application by validating key user flows through automated end-to-end testing.
user_story: As a developer, I want to ensure that the Spark application functions correctly in an integrated environment so that users experience a stable and reliable product.
acceptance_criteria: The e2e test should cover the main user flows in the Spark application.
The test should be executed successfully in the CI pipeline without errors.
The test should validate the integration of Spark SQL and Structured Streaming components.
The test should be documented with clear steps and expected outcomes.
technical_notes: Consider using existing test frameworks compatible with Spark, such as Apache Maven for Java/Scala or pytest for Python. Ensure that the test environment is properly set up to mimic production conditions. Pay attention to the dependencies specified in pom.xml and pyproject.toml to avoid version conflicts.
validation_notes: The ticket is well-structured with specific acceptance criteria, plausible related files, actionable technical notes, and includes change references.

title: add new UI for spark stream
business_req: Enhance user experience by providing a more intuitive and interactive interface for monitoring and managing Spark Streams.
user_story: As a data engineer, I want a user-friendly UI to monitor and manage Spark Streams so that I can efficiently oversee data processing tasks and quickly respond to any issues.
acceptance_criteria: The new UI for Spark Stream is accessible from the main dashboard.
The UI displays real-time data streams with minimal latency.
Users can filter and sort streams based on predefined criteria.
The UI is responsive and works across different devices and screen sizes.
All new UI components pass accessibility checks.
technical_notes: Consider leveraging existing UI components for consistency. Ensure that the new UI integrates seamlessly with the existing Spark Structured Streaming module. Pay attention to performance optimization to handle large-scale data streams efficiently.
validation_notes: The ticket is well-structured with specific acceptance criteria, plausible related files, actionable technical notes, and includes change references.

title: add emr support
business_req: Enable Spark to run efficiently on AWS EMR to leverage cloud scalability, reduce infrastructure overhead, and provide a managed environment for big data processing.
user_story: As a product manager, I want Spark to support EMR so that we can utilize AWS's managed Hadoop framework for scalable and efficient data processing, reducing the need for manual infrastructure management.
acceptance_criteria: Spark jobs can be submitted to AWS EMR and execute successfully.
Performance benchmarks show improved scalability and reduced infrastructure overhead compared to on-premise solutions.
Documentation is updated to include steps for deploying Spark on AWS EMR.
Automated tests cover EMR deployment scenarios and pass consistently.
technical_notes: Ensure compatibility with the latest EMR release. Consider using AWS SDKs for integration. Pay attention to IAM roles and permissions for accessing S3 and other AWS services. Test with different instance types to optimize cost and performance.
validation_notes: The ticket is well-structured with specific acceptance criteria, plausible related files, actionable technical notes, and includes change references.
